settings.editor: XCP Photography is committed to protecting your personal data and your right to privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, why we collect it, and how we protect it. Last updated: March 2026.

1. Who We Are

XCP Photography is operated by Xavier Campbell-Philips, a professional photographer based in Brighton, UK. For data protection purposes, Xavier Campbell-Philips is the Data Controller.

Contact: info@xcphotography.co.uk

2. What Data We Collect

We collect personal information you provide when you:

  • Submit a contact or enquiry form on this website
  • Email us directly
  • Book a shoot or discovery call

The data we collect includes: your name, email address, phone number, business name, project type, preferred dates, and any additional information you provide in the message field.

3. Why We Collect Your Data

We use your data solely to:

  • Respond to your enquiry and discuss your project
  • Manage bookings and deliver photography services
  • Send you a quote, invoice, or contract relevant to your project
  • Comply with our legal obligations

We do not use your data for marketing purposes without your explicit consent. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for their own marketing purposes.

4. Legal Basis for Processing

We process your data under the following lawful bases (UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018):

  • Contract performance — when your data is needed to carry out a booking or service
  • Legitimate interests — responding to your enquiry
  • Legal obligation — retaining financial records as required by HMRC

5. How Long We Keep Your Data

Enquiry and contact data is retained for up to 2 years. Booking and invoice records are retained for 6 years as required by UK tax law. Photographs produced under a commission are retained in accordance with the terms agreed in your contract.

6. Your Rights

Under UK GDPR, you have the right to:

  • Access the personal data we hold about you
  • Request correction of inaccurate data
  • Request deletion of your data (subject to legal retention requirements)
  • Object to processing of your data
  • Request restriction of processing
  • Data portability

To exercise any of these rights, email us at info@xcphotography.co.uk. We will respond within 30 days.

7. Third-Party Services

We use the following third-party services which may process your data:

  • Overture — our booking and CRM system. Your name, email, phone, and project details are sent to Overture when you submit the enquiry form. Overture's privacy policy is available at overturehq.com.
  • WordPress / web hosting — this website is hosted by a UK-based host. Form submissions are temporarily stored in WordPress before being forwarded to Overture and to our email.

We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, or any advertising tracking technology on this website.

8. Cookies

This website uses only essential cookies required for the site to function (for example, WordPress session cookies). No tracking or advertising cookies are used.

9. Security

We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure. Our website uses HTTPS encryption for all data transmission.

10. Changes to This Policy

We may update this privacy policy from time to time. The date at the top of this page reflects when it was last updated. Continued use of this website after any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

11. Complaints

If you believe your data has been handled incorrectly, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk.
Questions about this policy? Email us at info@xcphotography.co.uk and we will respond within 2 working days.
